Title: A kiwifruit (Actinidia deliciosa) R2R3-MYB transcription factor modulates chlorophyll and carotenoid accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cyclization branch from lycopene to β-carotene/β,β-xanthophyll branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes both ends of all-trans-lycopene to form β-carotene; with LCY-ε contributes to α-carotene formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In A. arguta fruit high at 20 DAFB, reduced at 50–70 DAFB, increased at 90 DAFB, then reduced; in A. macrosperma fruit increased progressively from 20 to 150 DAFB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NbLCY-β was upregulated in AdMYB7-overexpressing N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage; ripening; transient or stable AdMYB7 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activation dual-luciferase assay; promoter deletion assay; fruit expression profiling by RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased carotenoid accumulation and identified as a key carotenoid pathway step activated by AdMYB7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AdLCY-b promoter was strongly activated by the kiwifruit TF AdMYB7. 2. AdLCY-b expression correlated with pigment dynamics during kiwifruit fruit development, especially increasing in orange A. macrosperma fruit. 3. The gene encodes a key carotenoid-pathway enzyme leading to β-carotene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for boosting β-carotene/carotenoid accumulation, potentially in combination with its regulator AdMYB7.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene was a direct promoter target central to the study, but functional transgenics were performed on the regulator rather than direct LCY-b manipulation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; closest to AtMYB112/AtMYB108/AtMYB78 clade
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and modulation of degradation/homeostasis of chlorophyll and carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AdLCY-b; NbLCY-β; NbPSY; NbPDS; NbZDS; NbLCY-ε; chlorophyll-pathway genes including ALAD, PGBD, MGCH, MPECYC; NbGGR; NbSGR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AdLCY-b promoter directly; upregulates multiple carotenoid and chlorophyll metabolic genes in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller that modulates chlorophyll and carotenoid accumulation; direct activator of LCY-b and broader pathway inducer
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental stage/ripening; possibly light, ABA and stress context inferred from target promoter motifs, but not directly established for MYB7
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible indirect feedback within carotenoid pathway discussed; not directly established for AdMYB7 itself
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in transient and stable N. benthamiana overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at early fruit stage (20 DAFB) and again at ripe stage in A. arguta and A. macrosperma fruit; reduced during mid development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic N. benthamiana, expression varied among lines; moderate expression increased pigments whereas very high expression reduced pigments and stunted growth
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit development/ripening; Agrobacterium leaf infiltration; stable transformation under glasshouse long-day conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase promoter activation assay; EMSA; transient overexpression; stable overexpression; RT-qPCR; HPLC pigment profiling; RNA-seq transcriptomics
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modulates chlorophyll and carotenoid accumulation; moderate overexpression can increase pigments, while excessive overexpression reduces pigments and causes stunting
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AdMYB7 strongly activated the AdLCY-b promoter and physically bound it in EMSA. 2. AdMYB7 expression in kiwifruit fruit was associated with chlorophyll- and carotenoid-containing stages. 3. Overexpression in N. benthamiana altered chlorophyll and carotenoid levels and induced carotenoid/chlorophyll pathway genes. 4. Excessive expression caused growth retardation and reduced pigment accumulation, indicating dosage-dependent effects.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target to tune carotenoid and chlorophyll traits in fruit, but deployment should control expression level/tissue specificity to avoid growth defects and pigment loss at high expression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High transgene expression caused stunted growth and reduced chlorophyll/carotenoid accumulation; pleiotropic effects likely due to broad transcriptional activity
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000, 2×100 bp paired-end); accession number NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study suggests direct regulation of carotenoid biosynthesis and broader control of chloroplast/thylakoid organization and chlorophyll metabolism, including possible activation of SGR1 linked to chlorophyll degradation.
